message:
feat(pager,core): separate conflict surface from commit surface for concurrent WAL growth

Introduce `pending_conflict_pages()` on the TransactionHandle trait to
distinguish the set of pages that must participate in MVCC first-committer-
wins (FCW) tracking from the full commit surface.  Pure WAL growth
transactions synthesize a page-1 rewrite at commit time solely for header
metadata (change counter, page count); this rewrite is performed under the
serialized commit critical section and does not represent user-visible data
overlap.  Including it in the FCW surface falsely serializes disjoint
concurrent growth writers.

The new method filters out synthetic page-1 when the transaction is
concurrent WAL mode, has no freelist mutations, and no explicit page-1
writes.  All concurrent commit planning call-sites in Connection now call
`pending_conflict_pages()` instead of `pending_commit_pages()` and variable
names are updated accordingly.

The disjoint-growth-writer test is rewritten to assert that two writers
allocating separate data pages no longer conflict on page 1 in the FCW
surface while still preserving their real data pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
